critic_raw:
CRITIQUE:
1. PROMOTION_CAMPAIGN_CONFOUND: Sports event campaign with preferred creator placement launched week 2 of rollout — independent watch-time driver.
2. MIXED_ENTRYPOINT_MEASUREMENT: Notification sessions mixed with home-feed sessions; cannot attribute watch time to ranker.
3. NO_LOG_REPLAY_COMPARISON: No counterfactual — both rankers not replayed on the same requests.
defender_raw:
DEFENSE (seeing critique):
On promotion_campaign_confound: Conceded. The campaign is a direct independent mechanism for increased watch time.
On mixed_entrypoint_measurement: Conceded. Notification sessions have a different discovery mechanism and inflating aggregate metrics.
On no_log_replay_comparison: Conceded. Without a fixed-traffic replay, model quality and traffic composition are entangled.

The new model may still be better for ordinary home-feed traffic. The evidence to establish this requires entrypoint separation and log replay.
adjudication_raw: All three issues conceded in round 1. Empirical test required. The model may be better on ordinary home-feed traffic; aggregate lift is confounded. Verdict: empirical_test_agreed.

debate_rounds.defender_response: All three conceded. Model may still be better on ordinary traffic.

empirical_test.measure: Watch-time on home-feed-originated sessions excluding event traffic; log replay of both rankers on same request set
empirical_test.success_criterion: Lift disappears or becomes negligible on home-feed sessions with promotion effects removed
empirical_test.failure_criterion: New ranker maintains substantial watch-time improvement on home-feed sessions in event-neutral log replay
